steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A main-idea question asks what the passage is primarily about. The passage introduces artificial leaves, explains how they work, states they are "not yet commercially viable," and describes ongoing research to fix that. The main idea must capture both the barrier and the ongoing effort to overcome it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Continued research and development is needed before the devices can be widely used': Accurately captures the passage's central tension: the technology is promising but not yet viable, so research continues. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The recent increase in commercial use has encouraged research': Contradicts the passage, which states the technology is not yet commercially viable — there is no "recent increase in commercial use." ✗
  • Choice C — 'Artificial leaves were developed to store energy for long-term commercial use': Misrepresents the purpose. The passage says artificial leaves mimic photosynthesis and generate hydrogen/oxygen gas; long-term storage is one challenge being researched, not the original design goal. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Artificial leaves split water molecules more efficiently than plants do': The passage makes no efficiency comparison between artificial leaves and real plants. This detail does not appear in the text. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
